title: Environmental Regulations, Air and Water Pollution, and Infant Mortality in India
notes: EVENT STUDY REVIEWED. Pre-trends problematic: CS-NT/CS-NYT/SA show large negative values at h=-4,-5 (-40 to -60). Post-treatment positive but oscillating across estimators. Greenstone & Hanna (2014). Stata: xi: reg e_spm_mean scaprange scappolicy tauSCL tauSCR catrange catconvpolicy tauCATL tauCATR lit_urban mean i.city i.year [aw=pop_urban], cluster(city). Main outcome=SPM air pollution. Treatment=catalytic converter policy (staggered by city). catconvpolicy = post-adoption * within-event-window[-7,9]. Regression jointly controls for SCAP policy. Sample restricted to cities with >=3yr pre+post for BOTH policies. N=1172 (150 missing lit_urban/mean auto-dropped). Data built via build_analysis_data.R from combined.dta. tauSCR and tauCATR dropped for collinearity with city+year FEs. SE difference (R:11.93 vs Stata:12.59) due to feols singleton removal. CS-DID does NOT control for SCAP — results differ from TWFE by design. | Outcome selection: Rule (ii) largest N. Main DiD finding is energy poverty (SPM) with full county-year panel (N~45k). Mortality analysis uses a 2-step approach with only N=16 counties as units, too small for standard DiD. Audit confirmed.
